transcript: where we got a lawyer who i wrote the medical apartment complex people and you could end up dead from that right it is my second semester of my first year of medical school and our first exam is in three days i'm so behind on material i thought i would document my journey of cramming for this exam this semester we don't have that many exams so each exam counts towards a bigger portion of our grade which is honestly quite scary today is saturday my exam is on Tuesday and I have Monday off, so I have three full days to study for this exam, which I think is more than enough time if I use it productively. This semester we're learning about cardiovascular, renal, and respiratory systems, which I have learned before in my master's program, so hopefully a lot of it is review, but I also forgot about a lot of things. The test questions come from these list of objectives and some professors do a good job at telling you specifically what you need to know so I'm just going through this list and writing down some notes Thank you. i am determined to get through 992 anki cards today i'm not sleeping until i get through all of this i don't know if i'm delusional for thinking this is possible, but I'm going to try to make it happen. 다음 영상에서 만나요. 안 돼요 안 돼요 서두르지는 않을게요 운명을 믿어볼래요 우린 너무 다르지만 맞춰보는 그 속도 나와�� 노력이 없다면 사랑하길 okay so i didn't go through all of my anki cards yesterday only because some lectures i don't feel like the anki cards are useful and also some professors changed this year so the anki decks that i have are not up to date but i still ended up reviewing all the material so i am not hating myself too much right now I feel like I've been sick for the past month it just keeps coming back Thank you. 다음 영상에서 만나요. what if i just gave up what if i just dropped out i need to focus so this exam can be widely divided into three topics the first one is hemostasis primary secondary hemostasis and then the various diseases that affect hemostasis along with the drugs to treat those second one is all about anemias and porphyrias so we have microcytic normocytic macrocytic etiology pathogenesis clinical presentations and treatments of all of the different types of anemias and then And the last topic is on leukemias and lymphomas.
